requirements.text: Selecting a file shows its graph neighbors (from /graph) as a visual adjacency/traversal view.
planned_checks: GraphView.tsx fetches GET /graph?path=<path> when a path is submitted via the form or supplied via ?path= search param.

GraphView.tsx renders a center node (selected path) plus one row per returned neighbor (path, edgeType, weight, hop), with data-testid="graph-neighbor" per row and an SVG connector -- a real rendered adjacency view, not raw JSON dump.
GraphView.test.tsx 'renders neighbors after selecting a file' test: mocks fetch, types+submits a path, asserts graph-center-path text and graph-neighbor rows' content.

GraphView.test.tsx 'deep-links via ?path= search param' test: mounts with MemoryRouter initialEntries=['/graph?path=docs/x.md'] and asserts fetch is called + neighbors render without manual submission.

requirements.text: Test spec: component test mocking a /graph response, asserting the expected number of neighbor nodes render.
planned_checks: GraphView.test.tsx mocks fetch to resolve { path, neighbors: [3 entries] } and asserts screen.getAllByTestId('graph-neighbor') has length 3.

A second count-varying case (0 neighbors) asserts graph-no-neighbors renders instead, and getAllByTestId('graph-neighbor') throws/queryAllByTestId returns length 0 -- confirms the count assertion is meaningful (not hard-coded).

requirements.text: Consistency with established 6.1.2 conventions (self-imposed via architecture-discovery.md, not from the issue directly, but load-bearing for reviewability).
planned_checks: Same idle/loading/error/success state machine and data-testid naming scheme (graph-* mirroring query-*).
No new npm dependency added (checked via git diff of ui/package.json -- expected empty).

requirements.text: Error handling for a failed /graph call.
planned_checks: GraphView.test.tsx 'renders an error message when the /graph call fails' test: fetch resolves ok:false, asserts data-testid=graph-error with role=alert renders the error body text.

build_gates: npm run build (tsc -b && vite build) exits 0 inside ui/
npm test (vitest run) exits 0 inside ui/, including all new GraphView.test.tsx cases and pre-existing QueryView.test.tsx / App.test.tsx cases (no regression)
